description: The merchant has answered the agent's earlier label question with a URL (the re-plan path after ask_operator). The agent must attach the label to the open return with attach_return_label and send the customer the label link - not re-ask the merchant, not escalate, and not leave the label out of the reply.

messages.contentText: The linen curtains from order #11111 don't match my walls at all. I'd like to return them - could you send me a prepaid shipping label?
Return #11111-R1 opened on order 9000011111 for 1x Linen Curtains. Asked the merchant for a return label URL.

simulateToolResults.result: Attached the return label to return #11111-R1 on order 9000011111. Send the customer the label link in your reply so they can ship the items back: https://labels.example.com/rma-11111.pdf
Reply sent to customer via email.

instruction:
Reply to the customer about their return.

The store owner answered your question "Can you reply with a return label URL for the curtains return on order #11111?" with: "https://labels.example.com/rma-11111.pdf". Use this to draft the customer reply — do not ask again. The answer is a return label URL: call attach_return_label with the order_id and this URL, then include the link in your reply to the customer. The return itself is already open - do NOT call create_return or create_exchange again.
